workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Can you work with me on consumption trend for Lucerne Publishing? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-02-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:13:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
